summary: D-120 static review complete. Verified full localization of /app/frontend/app/invite/page.tsx: all hardcoded Norwegian UI strings replaced with t('invite_form.*') calls, ERROR_MESSAGES record replaced by ERROR_CODE_KEYS module-level mapping + local errMsg() closure inside InvitePageInner, useLocale() hook destructured with const { t, locale: pageLocale, setLocale: setPageLocale } = useLocale(), validate-success effect calls setPageLocale(body.invite.locale as Locale) under the if-guard `if (body.invite.locale && body.invite.locale !== pageLocale)` after setLocale(null), eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps comment present before `}, [token]);`. Master-password helper uses .split('{subdomain}').map(...) for styled span interpolation. Provisioning hint uses .replace('{action}', `"${t('invite_form.live_action')}"`). All 28 invite_form.* keys present in all 4 locale files (no/sv/da/en). i18n-sync-lint reports exactly 1483 keys × 4 languages (was 1455 in D-119, +28). yarn tsc --noEmit, yarn lint:all (5 passes green), and yarn build all PASS.

hardcoded_norwegian_check.method: Scanned /app/frontend/app/invite/page.tsx for all 26 banned Norwegian phrases listed in the review_request
hardcoded_norwegian_check.result: All 26 phrases absent from rendered/JSX strings. Only 4 occurrences total — 3 inside `//` comments (lines 21, 27, 269) and 1 inside a JSX `{/* ... */}` multi-line comment (line 476, continuation line where the script's startswith check failed but visual inspection confirms it's a JSX comment block from line 474-478). Zero hardcoded UI strings remain.

uselocale_hook.destructure_line_106: const { t, locale: pageLocale, setLocale: setPageLocale } = useLocale();

error_code_keys_mapping.shape: Record<string, string> mapping 12 codes to invite_form.* keys

validate_success_locale_switch.eslint_disable_comment: Line 179: // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps (immediately before `}, [token]);` on line 180)

interpolations.helper_master_password: Lines 437-450: uses .split('{subdomain}').map((part, idx, arr) => ...) wrapping the subdomain in a styled <span className='font-mono text-white/65'>. Each of no/sv/da/en helper_master_password contains exactly one {subdomain} placeholder.
interpolations.provisioning_hint: Lines 482-485: uses .replace('{action}', `"${t('invite_form.live_action')}"`). Each locale provisioning_hint contains exactly one {action} placeholder.
interpolations.err_generic: Each locale err_generic contains exactly one {code} placeholder (NO: 'Feil: {code}', SV: 'Fel: {code}', DA: 'Fejl: {code}', EN: 'Error: {code}').

locale_files_28_keys.method: Python check: flattened keys with prefix 'invite_form.' against the required 28-key list
locale_files_28_keys.en: 28/28 present — verified no Norwegian text leaked (subtitle: 'Activate your account', submit: 'Activate account', live_action: 'OK, continue', etc.)
locale_files_28_keys.placeholder_email_locale_specific: NO: navn@firma.no | SV: namn@foretag.se | DA: navn@firma.dk | EN: name@company.com — matches review_request note about intentional locale-specific examples

static_checks.yarn_lint_all: PASS — 5 lint passes green: D-077 tenant-env-manifest, coverage-matrix (51 routes, 44 exempt), i18n-sync (1483 keys × 4 languages, 1288 t() calls verified, 56 dynamic exempt), D-078a PII, D-105 dedup
static_checks.yarn_build: PASS (Done in 25.07s, exit 0). /invite route = 4.6 kB / 209 kB First Load JS

critical_code_review_comments: All D-120 requirements met. Code is clean, well-commented, and consistent.

Minor stylistic note (not blocking): The errMsg() closure is recreated on every render inside InvitePageInner. Since ERROR_CODE_KEYS is module-level and t() comes from useLocale, this is correct and cheap, but if React DevTools profiling ever flags it, wrapping in useCallback([t]) would memoize across re-renders without behavior change.

Minor observation (not blocking): line 476 contains the literal 'OK, gå videre' inside a multi-line JSX `{/* ... */}` comment block (continuation line). This is dead text in a comment and not user-facing, but if you want to be 100% Norwegian-free in source, you could rewrite the comment to reference the i18n key (`invite_form.live_action`) instead of the original Norwegian copy.

Suggestion (future): Consider lifting helper_master_password to support multiple placeholders via a shared `tInterpolate(key, vars)` util — currently each interpolation pattern is hand-coded. Not required for D-120.
context_for_next_testing_agent: D-120 verified via static analysis only (per user prohibition on yarn dev / E2E browser tests in this kubernetes pod). yarn tsc / lint:all / build all green. i18n-sync-lint now reports 1483 keys × 4 languages (was 1455, +28 invite_form.* keys). All hardcoded Norwegian UI strings in /app/frontend/app/invite/page.tsx replaced with t() calls. useLocale() hook + setPageLocale-on-validate-success + ERROR_CODE_KEYS + errMsg() closure + {subdomain}/{action}/{code} interpolations all verified. EN translations contain zero Norwegian residue. Previous reports: iteration_19.json (D-117), iteration_20.json (D-118), iteration_21.json (D-119).
